BLOG.HERO.TITLE: Production-Ready Microservices Architecture
BLOG.HERO.SUBTITLE: Static content on AWS, /api traffic to on-premise Kubernetes and decoupled services behind an Ingress.
BLOG.STATUS: 🚧 Architecture in evolution — aligned with the target diagram
BLOG.INTRO.TEXT: The platform separates frontend delivery from the business runtime. Users enter through the public domain, receive static assets from AWS, and only API requests travel to the Kubernetes cluster.
BLOG.DIAGRAM.TITLE: Architecture diagram
BLOG.DIAGRAM.CAPTION: AWS serves static content, Route53 and CloudFront distribute public traffic, and Kubernetes handles the microservices layer.
BLOG.TECH.AWS.TITLE: AWS Cloud
BLOG.TECH.AWS.DESC: Public layer used to serve the domain, frontend and static assets.
BLOG.TECH.AWS.USE: Provides global distribution, caching and a clean entry point for static content.
BLOG.TECH.INGRESS.TITLE: Nginx Ingress
BLOG.TECH.INGRESS.DESC: Cluster entry point that routes /api traffic to internal services.
BLOG.TECH.INGRESS.USE: Centralizes HTTP access and keeps public exposure separate from microservices.
BLOG.TECH.KUBERNETES.TITLE: Kubernetes
BLOG.TECH.KUBERNETES.DESC: Runtime environment for the business layer and its independent services.
BLOG.TECH.KUBERNETES.USE: Orchestrates deployment, scaling and availability of the microservices.
BLOG.TECH.MICROSERVICES.TITLE: Microservices
BLOG.TECH.MICROSERVICES.DESC: Separate services for user, cart, product, order and mail.
BLOG.TECH.MICROSERVICES.USE: Each service owns a focused responsibility and can evolve independently.
BLOG.TECH.AUTH.TITLE: Hybrid Auth
BLOG.TECH.AUTH.DESC: Hybrid authentication with local database users and Google as an external provider.
BLOG.TECH.AUTH.USE: Combines internal user control with a social login flow.
BLOG.TECH.CONFIG.TITLE: Secrets and App Config
BLOG.TECH.CONFIG.DESC: Sensitive configuration and environment settings kept outside the codebase.
BLOG.TECH.CONFIG.USE: Reduces coupling and keeps deployments consistent across environments.
BLOG.FLOW.TITLE: Architecture Flow
BLOG.FLOW.TEXT: The user enters through the public domain, receives static content from AWS, and /api requests go through the Ingress to the services running in the cluster. Hybrid auth and external configuration complete the path.
BLOG.ROADMAP.TITLE: Project Status
BLOG.ROADMAP.ITEM1: ✔ Public delivery of static assets on AWS
BLOG.ROADMAP.ITEM4: ✔ Hybrid authentication with Google and database
BLOG.ROADMAP.ITEM5: ✔ Configuration through Secrets and App Config
